item.title: dart/Flutter 中的异常处理和日志记录 - Talker | Exception handling and logging in dart/Flutter - Talker
item.description:
斯坦尼斯拉夫·伊林

发表于异常，异常，异常……
我们在应用程序的开发和支持过程中经常看到它们。

其中一些是我们所期待的，有些是我们甚至没有想到的。 作为程序员，你能做的最糟糕的事情就是不去考虑捕捉和跟踪错误。 让我们弄清楚如何做到这一点......

飞镖中的异常处理

例如，我们有这个代码。 这是来自某些服务的逻辑调用。

 final result = service . makeLogic (); print ( result );

如果我们在服务中遇到错误会发生什么？ 答案很简单：程序崩溃并以错误结束

应该避免这样的时刻。 为此，dart 有一个 try-catch 结构。

 String ? result ; try { result = service . makeLogic (); } on Exception catch ( e ) { print ( e ); } result ??= 'Can`t get result' ; print ( result );


在这种情况下，程序将能够继续执行并正确终止

异常跟踪

我们已经想出了如何捕捉错误，但是如何进行跟踪和错误分析呢？

它可以跟踪 Flutter 应用程序中的错误。

甚至有一些项目可以制作自己的分析系统。
在初创公司中，这些工具可能会经常变化。 你想每次都手动写这样的东西吗？

 try { // your code... } on Exception catch ( exception , stackTrace ) { Crashlytics . instance . recordError ( exception , stackTrace , reason: exception . message ); Sentry . captureException ( exception , stackTrace: stackTrace ); YourOwnAnalytics . sendError ( exception , stackTrace: stackTrace ); ScaffoldMessenger . of ( context ) . showSnackBar ( SnackBar ( content: Text ( exception . toString ()))); Logger . error ( 'Something Service exception' , exception , stackTrace ); }

看起来好可怕🤢
但是我们如何解决这个问题呢？

健谈者


我向您介绍我的库Talker的稳定版本

它是一个用于存储和分发应用程序调试信息的系统。

开始使用

您可以从Talker 文档站点开始

但我也会在这里告诉你

对于Dart应用程序，您需要将talker依赖项添加到您的pubspec.yaml

 dependencies : talker : ^1.0.0

对于Flutter应用程序，您可以将talker_flutter或talker依赖项添加到您的pubspec.yaml

 dependencies : talker_flutter : ^1.0.0

有什么区别？

像TalkerScreen或TalkerRouteObserver

便于使用

首次启动所需要做的就是导入依赖项并创建 Talker 类的实例

import 'package:talker/talker.dart' ; void main ( ){ final talker = Talker (); talker . info ( 'I`m alive 😄' ); }

您可以在代码中的任何地方使用 Talker 实例 简单而简洁的语法将帮助您解决这个问题

final talker = Talker (); // Handle exceptions and errors try { // your code... } on Exception catch ( e , st ) { talker . handle ( e , st , 'Exception in ...' ); } // Make logs talker . log ( 'App is started' ), talker . error ( 'App is started' ), talker . waring ( 'App is started' ),

您可以在此处或GitHub中获取更多示例

颤振初始化

设置 Talker 以在runZonedGuarded onError()回调中侦听您的应用程序未捕获的异常

void main ( ) { final talker = Talker (); runZonedGuarded ( () => runApp ( BaseEample ( talker: talker )), ( Object error , StackTrace stack ) { talker . handle ( error , stack , 'Uncaught app exception' ); }, ); }

这样初始化后，所有未捕获的错误和异常都会发送给Talker进行处理

观察员

现在我将告诉您如何从文章的开头解决问题的情况

使用 Talker 库，您可以简化多个服务中的错误处理

现在这个可怕的意大利面条代码被替换了

try { // your code... } on Exception catch ( exception , stackTrace ) { Crashlytics . instance . recordError ( exception , stackTrace , reason: exception . message ); Sentry . captureException ( exception , stackTrace: stackTrace ); YourOwnAnalytics . sendError ( exception , stackTrace: stackTrace ); ScaffoldMessenger . of ( context ) . showSnackBar ( SnackBar ( content: Text ( exception . toString ()))); Logger . error ( 'Something Service exception' , exception , stackTrace ); }

对此->

 try { // your code... } on Exception catch ( e , st ) { talker . handle ( e , st , 'Something Service exception' ); }

要将分析发送到其他服务，只需添加一个观察者

class ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver extends TalkerObserver { ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver (); @override Function ( TalkerException e ) get onException => ( e ) { Crashlytics . instance . recordError ( e . exception , stack: e . stackTrace , reason: e . message ); Sentry . captureException ( e . exception , stackTrace: e . stackTrace ); YourOwnAnalytics . sendError ( e . exception , stackTrace: e . stackTrace ); }; }

并在谈话者的设置中实现这个观察者

final talker = Talker ( observers: [ ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver (), ], );

现在您的所有应用程序异常事件都将来到 ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver 观察者，并将被重定向到必要的服务
是不是很漂亮？ 您可以在Talker 文档站点的自定义主题中阅读更多信息

除了上述之外，Talker 还可以做很多其他的事情。 自定义日志、共享日志报告等。

我在这个包上投入了大量的精力和时间。 开发和测试它 4 个月。 现在我正在将它实施到我现有的生产项目中。 它工作得很好，开发人员对此非常满意。

如果您在项目中尝试并报告任何缺点，我也会非常高兴。 如需文档，请访问我们的个人网站

非常感谢您的关注！！！

菲利波·费兰多·达米拉诺 - May 31 5月31日

Zoltán Szőgyényi - Zoltán Szőgyényi - May 13 5月13日



原文:

Stanislav Ilin

Posted on Jun 4

Exceptions, Exceptions, Exceptions...
We see them constantly during development and support of Applications.

Some of them we expect, and some we haven't even thought about. The worst thing you can do as a programmer is not to think about catching and tracking errors. Let's figure out how to do it...

Exceptions handling in dart

For example, we have this code. This is a call of logic from some service.

final result = service.makeLogic();
print(result);

What will happen if we get an error in the service? The answer is simple: program crashes and ends with an error

Such moments should be avoided. To do this, dart has a try-catch construct.

String? result;
try { result = service.makeLogic();
} on Exception catch (e) { print(e);
}
result ??= 'Can`t get result';
print(result);


In this case, the program will be able to continue execution and will terminate correctly

Exceptions Tracking

We've figured out how to catch bugs, but what to do with tracking and error analysis?

There is a Crashlytics service from firebase for Flutter applications.

It can track errors in your Flutter application.

Crashlytics, Sentry, AppsFlyer and many others. There are even projects that make their own analytical system.
In startups, these tools can change frequently. Are you want to manually write something like this every time?

 try { // your code... } on Exception catch (exception, stackTrace) { Crashlytics.instance .recordError(exception, stackTrace, reason: exception.message); Sentry.captureException(exception, stackTrace: stackTrace); YourOwnAnalytics.sendError(exception, stackTrace: stackTrace); ScaffoldMessenger.of(context) .showSnackBar(SnackBar(content: Text(exception.toString()))); Logger.error('Something Service exception', exception, stackTrace); }

It looks terrible🤢
But how we can fix this case?

Talker


I present to you the stable release of my library Talker

Talker is advanced exception handling and logging tool for dart/flutter applications.
It is a system for storing and distributing debugging information of your application.

Get started

You can get started at Talker documentation site

But I'll tell you here too

For Dart applications you need add talker dependency to your pubspec.yaml

dependencies: talker: ^1.0.0

For Flutter applications you can add talker_flutter or talker dependency to your pubspec.yaml

dependencies: talker_flutter: ^1.0.0

What's difference ?

talker_flutter has advanced features that's convenient to use in the Flutter application


like TalkerScreen or TalkerRouteObserver

talker package working both for dart and flutter applications

Easy to use

All you need for the first start is to import the dependency and create an instance of the Talker class

import 'package:talker/talker.dart';
void main(){ final talker = Talker(); talker.info('I`m alive 😄');
}

You can use Talker instance everywhere in your code Simple and concise syntax will help you with this

final talker = Talker();
// Handle exceptions and errors
try { // your code...
} on Exception catch (e, st) { talker.handle(e, st, 'Exception in ...');
} // Make logs
talker.log('App is started'),
talker.error('App is started'),
talker.waring('App is started'),

More examples you can get there or in GitHub

Flutter initialization

Setup Talker to listen your app uncaught exceptions in runZonedGuarded onError() callback

void main() { final talker = Talker(); runZonedGuarded( () => runApp(BaseEample(talker: talker)), (Object error, StackTrace stack) { talker.handle(error, stack, 'Uncaught app exception'); }, );
}

After such initialization, all uncaught errors and exceptions will be sent to Talker for processing

Observers

And now I will tell you how to fix the problematic situation from the beginning of the article

Using the Talker library, you can simplify error handling in several services

And now this terrible spaghetti code is replaced

 try { // your code... } on Exception catch (exception, stackTrace) { Crashlytics.instance .recordError(exception, stackTrace, reason: exception.message); Sentry.captureException(exception, stackTrace: stackTrace); YourOwnAnalytics.sendError(exception, stackTrace: stackTrace); ScaffoldMessenger.of(context) .showSnackBar(SnackBar(content: Text(exception.toString()))); Logger.error('Something Service exception', exception, stackTrace); }

To this ->

try { // your code...
} on Exception catch (e, st) { talker.handle(e, st, 'Something Service exception');
}

To send analytics to other services, just add an observer

class ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver extends TalkerObserver { ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver(); @override Function(TalkerException e) get onException => (e) { Crashlytics.instance .recordError(e.exception, stack: e.stackTrace, reason: e.message); Sentry.captureException(e.exception, stackTrace: e.stackTrace); YourOwnAnalytics.sendError(e.exception, stackTrace: e.stackTrace); };
}

And implement this observer in talker's settings

final talker = Talker( observers: [ ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver(), ],
);

Now all your app exception events will come to ExceptionsAnalyticsTalkerObserver observer and will be redirected to the necessary services
Isn 't it beautiful? 😌

You can read more in Customization topic at Talker documentation site

In addition to the above, Talker can do a lot of other things. Customize logs, share a log report, etc.

I put a lot of effort and time into this package. Develop and test it for 4 months. Now I am implementing it into my existing production projects. It works great and the developers are very happy with it.

❤️I will be very pleased if you promote this project and put a star on GitHub and pub.dev

I will also be very glad if you try it in your project and report any shortcomings. For documentation, visit our personal website

Thank you very much for your attention!!!

Filippo Ferrando Damillano - May 31

Zoltán Szőgyényi - May 13
item.author: frezyx

item.title: 与同一站点上的人交谈🌎 | Chrome Extension Chrome 扩展程序 | Talk to people who are on the same site 🌎 | Chrome Extension
item.description:
你好编码员👋

在过去的几周里，我一直在为Chromium Base Browsers开发一个网络扩展🌎。

它能做什么？ 一个扩展程序，允许您通过互联网与与您在同一网站/网页中的人交谈，假设他们也安装了该应用程序。

你能想象这是多么令人兴奋🤩。 当我想到这个想法时，我也有同样的感觉。

拥有无限可能🤩

  • 与志趣相投的人交谈
  • 在电子商务网站上进行任何交易之前与人们交谈。

我该如何使用它？ 所以，就在昨天，我已经提交了它以供审核，它可能需要 3 天左右才能在 Chrome 网上商店上线。

所以保持联系并订阅，一旦它上线，我会发布一篇文章。

谢谢，
快乐编码



原文:

Hello Coders 👋

For past few weeks, I've been working on a web extension 🌎 for Chromium Base Browsers.

What it does? 🤔

An extension that will allow you to talk to people over the internet, who are in the same website/webpage as you are in, assuming they've installed the app as well.

Can you imagine how exciting 🤩 this is. I felt the same when this idea stricken my mind.

With unlimited possibilities 🤩

  • Talk to people with similar interests
  • Talk to people before making any transaction on an eCommerce site.

How can I use it? 🤔

So, just yesterday I've submitted it for the review, it may take up-to 3days or so to get live on Chrome web store.

So stay connected and subscribed, I'll post an article once it goes live.

Thanks,
happy coding

item.title: 在 React 中上传之前预览文件 | Preview File before uploading in React
item.description:
本文将解释如何在 React 中构建适用于图像和视频的文件预览器。 在本文的帮助下，您可以创建自己的文件并为其他文件提供支持。

本文仅展示图像和视频的预览。

演示

创建FilePreviewer组件

首先，让我们创建一个文件components/FilePreviewer.js ，然后我们需要在该文件中导入两个东西useState和useRef 。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState , useRef } from " react " ;

创建一个FilePreviewer函数并将其导出为默认值。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js // .... export default function FilePreviewer () {}

现在我们为文件选择器渲染 UI，其中将有两个按钮。 一个用于选择文件，另一个用于清除文件输入。 让我们看看它会是什么样子。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState , useRef } from " react " ; export default function FilePreviewer () { return ( < div > < h1 > Preview Image/Video </ h1 > < div className = "btn-container" > < input type = "file" accept = "image/*, video/*" hidden /> < button className = "btn" > Choose </ button > < button className = "btn" > x </ button > </ div > < div className = "preview" > < img src = "" alt = "" /> < video controls src = "" ></ video > </ div > </ div > ); }

这只是一个入门代码，我将在其中添加更多内容。 首先，了解发生了什么。 正如您在btn-container类中看到的那样，有三个输入。 一种用于选择文件，但我不会使用标准文件输入，因为当用户通过标准输入选择文件时，它会显示我不想要的文件的名称（如下面的屏幕截图所示）。

处理文件输入按钮

我创建了一个新按钮来选择文件。 为了完成这项工作，我们需要为文件输入创建一个参考 (ref)。 然后处理onChange事件，它看起来像这样。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState , useRef } from " react " ; export default function FilePreviewer () { return ( // inside .btn-container < input ref = { filePicekerRef } accept = "image/*, video/*" onChange = { previewFile } type = "file" hidden /> // ... ); }

稍后我们将创建previewFile函数来处理文件选择。

创建自定义文件输入按钮

现在，由于我隐藏了原始文件输入按钮，我们需要创建自己的按钮。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState , useRef } from " react " ; export default function FilePreviewer () { return ( // inside .btn-container < button className = "btn" onClick = { () => filePicekerRef . current . click () } > Choose </ button > // ... ); }

在此，当用户单击此按钮时，我只是通过ref触发文件输入按钮。

文件选择

因为我们正在处理两个文件（图像和视频）。 我们需要为imagePreview和videoPreview创建两个状态。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState , useRef } from " react " ; export default function FilePreviewer () { const [ imagePreview , setImagePreview ] = useState ( null ); const [ videoPreview , setVideoPreview ] = useState ( null ); return ( // ... ); }

现在是时候创建一个filePreview函数了。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js export default function FilePreviewer () { // ... function previewFile ( e ) { // Reading New File (open file Picker Box) const reader = new FileReader (); // Gettting Selected File (user can select multiple but we are choosing only one) const selectedFile = e . target . files [ 0 ]; if ( selectedFile ) { reader . readAsDataURL ( selectedFile ); } // As the File loaded then set the stage as per the file type reader . onload = ( readerEvent ) => { if ( selectedFile . type . includes ( " image " )) { setImagePreview ( readerEvent . target . result ); } else if ( selectedFile . type . includes ( " video " )) { setVideoPreview ( readerEvent . target . result ); } }; } // ... }

我知道这太多了，所以让我们分解一下。 我正在使用FileReader来处理文件选择。

  • 我创建了一个名为reader的实例。
  • 然后我们从输入字段中获取selectedFile （我只针对一个文件，用户可以选择多个文件但我只处理一个文件）。
  • 如果用户选择了一个文件，则将其读取为Data URLs 。
  • 加载文件后，检查文件类型并相应地设置图像和视频。

预览文件

文件选择完成后，我们需要向用户预览文件。 为此，我已经创建了一个名为.preview的容器，其中有两个元素img和video 。 现在我们需要有条件地渲染这些元素。 之后它们看起来像这样-

 // components/FilePreviewer.js < div className = "preview" > { imagePreview != null && < img src = { imagePreview } alt = "" /> } { videoPreview != null && < video controls src = { videoPreview } ></ video > } </ div >

清除输入字段

现在，如果用户想要清除输入字段或删除他选择的图像怎么办。 我们还没有实施。 为此，我之前创建了一个close按钮。 现在让我们为其添加功能。 当用户单击按钮时，它应该触发clearFiles函数。 所以让我们创建它。

 // components/FilePreviewer.js function clearFiles () { setImagePreview ( null ); setVideoPreview ( null ); }

这就是我们创建工作文件预览器所需的全部内容。 它可以预览图像和视频。

现在我们只需要在App.js中导入这个容器并渲染它。 您可以在以下沙箱中找到完整的代码

如果视频的尺寸很大，则渲染视频需要一点白色。 您可以为此设置加载状态。

下一步是什么？

现在，您可以更进一步并添加对其他文件（如文本、pdf 等）的支持。 您还可以添加对多个文件的支持，您可以做很多事情。



原文:

This article will explain how you can build a file previewer in React that works for images and videos. With this article's help, you can create your own and make support for other files.

This article only showcases the preview for an image and video.

Demo

Creating FilePreviewer Component

First, let's just create a file components/FilePreviewer.js after that we need to import two things in that file useState and useRef.

// components/FilePreviewer.js
import { useState, useRef } from "react";

Create a FilePreviewer function and export it as default.

// components/FilePreviewer.js
// ....
export default function FilePreviewer() {}

Now we render the UI for FIle Picker and in that there will be two buttons. One for selecting files and the other for clearing file input. Let's see how it's going to look like.

// components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState, useRef } from "react";
export default function FilePreviewer() { return ( <div> <h1>Preview Image/Video</h1> <div className="btn-container"> <input type="file" accept="image/*, video/*" hidden /> <button className="btn">Choose</button> <button className="btn">x</button> </div> <div className="preview"> <img src="" alt="" /> <video controls src=""></video> </div> </div> );
}

This is just a starter code, I am going to add more things to this. First, understand what is going on. As you can see inside the btn-container class there are three inputs. One for selecting files but I won't use standard file input because when the user selects the file by standard input it shows the name of the file which I don't want (as shown in the following screenshot).

Handling File Input button

I have created a new button to choose the file. To make this work we need to create a reference (ref) for file input. and handle the onChange event after that it will look something like this.

// components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState, useRef } from "react"; export default function FilePreviewer() { return ( // inside .btn-container <input ref={filePicekerRef} accept="image/*, video/*" onChange={previewFile} type="file" hidden /> // ... );
}

We will create the previewFile function in just a moment to handle the file selection.

Creating Custom File input Button

Now as I have hidden the original file input button we need to create our own.

// components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState, useRef } from "react"; export default function FilePreviewer() { return ( // inside .btn-container <button className="btn" onClick={()=> filePicekerRef.current.click()} > Choose </button> // ... );
}

In this, I am just triggering the file input button through ref when the user clicks this button.

File Selection

As we are handling two files (image and video). we need to create two states for that imagePreview and videoPreview.

// components/FilePreviewer.js import { useState, useRef } from "react"; export default function FilePreviewer() { const [imagePreview, setImagePreview] = useState(null); const [videoPreview, setVideoPreview] = useState(null); return ( // ... );
}

Now its' time to create a filePreview function.

// components/FilePreviewer.js export default function FilePreviewer() { // ... function previewFile(e) { // Reading New File (open file Picker Box) const reader = new FileReader(); // Gettting Selected File (user can select multiple but we are choosing only one) const selectedFile = e.target.files[0]; if (selectedFile) { reader.readAsDataURL(selectedFile); } // As the File loaded then set the stage as per the file type reader.onload = (readerEvent) => { if (selectedFile.type.includes("image")) { setImagePreview(readerEvent.target.result); } else if (selectedFile.type.includes("video")) { setVideoPreview(readerEvent.target.result); } }; } // ...
}

I know it's too much so let's break it down. I am using FileReader to handle the file selection.

  • I have created an instance called reader.
  • Then we are getting the selectedFile from an input field (I am targeting only one file, the user can select multiple files but I am handling only one file).
  • If the user has selected a file then read that as Data URLs.
  • When the file is loaded then check for the file type and set the image and video accordingly.

Preview the file

After file selection is done then we need to preview the file to the user. For that I have already created a container called .preview, In that, there were two elements img and video. Now we need to render these elements conditionally. and after that they will look like this-

// components/FilePreviewer.js  <div className="preview"> {imagePreview != null && <img src={imagePreview} alt="" />} {videoPreview != null && <video controls src={videoPreview}></video>}
</div>

Clear Input Field

Now, what if the user wants to clear the input field or remove the image he has selected. We haven't implemented that yet. To do that I've created a close button earlier. Now let's just add the functionality to it. When the user clicks on the button then it should fire clearFiles function. So let's just create it.

// components/FilePreviewer.js  function clearFiles() { setImagePreview(null); setVideoPreview(null);
}

That's all we need to create a working file Previewer. It can preview an image and a video.

Full Code of FilePreviewer.js 

// components/FilePreviewer.js 
import { useState, useRef } from "react"; export default function FilePreviewer() { // FIles States const [imagePreview, setImagePreview] = useState(null); const [videoPreview, setVideoPreview] = useState(null); // FIle Picker Ref because we are not useing the standard File picker input const filePicekerRef = useRef(null); function previewFile(e) { // Reading New File (open file Picker Box) const reader = new FileReader(); // Gettting Selected File (user can select multiple but we are choosing only one) const selectedFile = e.target.files[0]; if (selectedFile) { reader.readAsDataURL(selectedFile); } // As the File loaded then set the stage as per the file type reader.onload = (readerEvent) => { if (selectedFile.type.includes("image")) { setImagePreview(readerEvent.target.result); } else if (selectedFile.type.includes("video")) { setVideoPreview(readerEvent.target.result); } }; } function clearFiles() { setImagePreview(null); setVideoPreview(null); } return ( <div> <h1>Preview Image/Video</h1> <div className="btn-container"> <input ref={filePicekerRef} accept="image/*, video/*" onChange={previewFile} type="file" hidden /> <button className="btn" onClick={() => filePicekerRef.current.click()}> Choose </button> {(imagePreview || videoPreview) && ( <button className="btn" onClick={clearFiles}> x </button> )} </div> <div className="preview"> {imagePreview != null && <img src={imagePreview} alt="" />} {videoPreview != null && <video controls src={videoPreview}></video>} </div> </div> );
}

Now we just need to import this container in App.js and render it. App.js will look something like this.

// src/App.js import "./styles.css";
import FilePreviewer from "./components/FilePreviewer"; export default function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <FilePreviewer /> </div> );
}

You can find the full code in the following Sandbox

It takes a little white to render the video if the size of the video is large. You can setup a loading state for that.

What's Next?

Now after that you can take this further and add support for other files such as text, pdf, and others. You can also add support for multiple files and there are many things you can do.
item.author: j471n

item.title: 微服务反模式 | Microservices anti-patterns
item.description:
本篇博客主要讲构建微服务架构系统的反模式。

微服务架构在许多方面都有很大帮助。 可读性、可维护性、可扩展性、关注点分离等等。

另一方面，如果在微服务架构中应用一些反模式，这些优点可能会变成缺点，并且很难维护。

反模式是什么意思？

在软件工程和计算机科学中，反模式是一个描述如何不解决代码库中重复出现的问题的术语。 因此，它们总是被认为是系统设计中的不良做法。

不同的微服务架构反模式。

这是我使用过的一种反模式。

单体应用程序是指将应用程序的整个逻辑封装在一个单元或代码库中的系统。

有些人犯的错误之一是将大型单体应用程序的逻辑分配到几个小型单体应用程序中，并将其称为微服务架构。

事实上，这些小型单体应用程序最终会与其他人聊天，以了解大型单体应用程序的工作方式。

这些单一应用程序之一的任何微小变化都会导致传奇破裂。 并且需要对其他系统进行更改，这违背了微服务架构的目的。

微服务架构中的内聚意味着单个实体的组件如何捆绑在一起。

话虽如此，微服务不应该关心其他实体的组件。

当不相关的组件或功能不断地添加到特定的微服务中时，就会出现这种反模式。

当所有微服务都应该按特定顺序部署时，也会出现这种反模式，否则，微服务之间的传奇和闲聊就会破裂。

这也是由关注点分离引起的。

这是当多个微服务连接到同一个数据库时发生的反模式。

例如，如果一个微服务由于其内部逻辑需要更改特定模式，那么也连接到该数据库的微服务应该适应相应的更改。

当每个微服务中发生某些功能时，就会发生操作冗余。

假设，为了让一个动作在微服务中启动一个 saga，正在执行该动作的用户应该经过身份验证。 就此而言，用户应该被认证一次。

在这种情况下，拥有 API 网关变得非常方便，可以集中微服务之间的共享逻辑并协调它们之间的事件。

结论

了解微服务架构及其反模式非常重要。 尝试应用它们可能会增加很多复杂性，而不考虑应该如何构建它们的整体设计。



原文:

This blog mainly talks about the anti-patterns of building microservice architecture systems.

A microservice architecture helps a lot in many aspects. Readability, Maintainability, scalability, separation of concerns, and many more.

On the other hand, these pros might turn into cons if some anti-patterns are applied in a microservice architecture and it would be very difficult to maintain.

What does anti-pattern mean?

In software engineering and computer science, an anti-pattern is a term that describes how not to solve repetitive recurring issues in your codebase. Therefore they are always considered to be bad practices in the design of systems.

Different Microservices architecture anti-patterns.

1. Distributed Monolithic systems.

This is an anti-pattern that I have experience working with.

A monolithic app means a system that encapsulates the whole logic of the app in one unit or codebase.

One of the mistakes some people do is distributing the logic of that big monolithic app into several small monolithic apps and calling it a microservice architecture.

The fact is these small monolithic apps will eventually chat with others to achieve how the big monolithic app was working.

Any small change in one of these monolithic apps will cause the saga to break. And a change in other systems will be required, and that defeats the purpose of microservice architecture.

2. Cohesion chaos and coupling.

Cohesion in microservice architecture means the amount of how components of a single entity are tied together.

With that being said, a microservice should not care about the components of other entities.

This anti-pattern arises when unrelated components or functionalities are constantly being added to a specific microservice.

3. Dependency distortion.

This anti-pattern also arises when all of the microservices should be deployed in a specific order, otherwise, the saga and the chattiness between the microservices will break.

This is caused by the separation of concerns as well.

4. Shared schemas and datastores.

This is an anti-pattern that occurs when multiple microservices are connected to the same db.

For example, if one microservice needs to change a specific schema due to its internal logic, the microservice that is also connected to that database should adapt to the corresponding change.

4. Actions redundancy.

Action redundancy occurs when there are some functionalities that are occurring in each microservice.

Suppose that, in order for an action to start a saga in the microservices, the user who is doing the action should be authenticated. For that matter, the user should be authenticated once.

Having an API gateway becomes very handy in such situations to centralize shared logic between microservices and also to orchestrate the events between them.

Conclusion

Understanding microservice architecture and its anti-patterns are very important. A lot of complexity might be added trying to apply them without thinking of the overall design of how they should be built.
item.author: evanhameed99

item.title: 为什么开源项目应该包含运营透明度 | Why open source projects should embrace operational transparency
item.description:
本文由The Lead Dev和juliaferraioli.com交叉发布。

从操作系统到 CSS 框架，开源使创建者能够加速开发、向专家社区学习并整合经过彻底审查的代码。 开源的核心是与代码相关的自由，但开源项目是一个碰巧专注于代码的复杂系统。

在我的上一篇文章中，我们开始探索如何摆脱对开源许可的典型关注，转而开发一种能够捕捉额外复杂性的社会模型。 回顾一下，我们建立了开源的技术模型描述了你可以用它做什么和不能做什么，通常通过项目的许可证，而社会模型描述了开源如何与更大的生态系统交互（或不交互）和里面的人。 最初的文章讨论了根据项目在开源生态系统中的特定目标来捕捉项目的目的。

在第二篇文章中，我们将继续开发开源的社会模型，探索运营透明度的好处，并概述维护者应该致力于公开的一些关键特征。

运营透明度的好处

开源的一个关键好处是任何人都可以检查他们合并的代码，但是项目的运行方式对于项目的成功与功能本身同样重要。 随着开源占当今现代技术的90% 左右，让公众可以随时获取运营信息变得越来越重要，这样您就可以授权贡献者和用户根据对他们来说很重要的标准来评估项目。

开源项目的运营特征通常被认为是其背后团队的基础，但它们很少在维护者之外得到很好的沟通。 这是一个错失的机会，因为开源项目清楚、简洁、一致地表达了他们的运作方式，为贡献者和用户提供了他们在项目中正常工作所需的社会框架。

以下是项目应旨在公开的一些高优先级细节：

运行开源项目的一个重要部分是决定如何运行项目。 虽然“治理”是一个不断扩大的术语，但它主要关注的是如何制定决策以及谁来制定决策。 一个例子是 Node.js，它公开分享它遵循寻求共识的决策模型。

随着越来越多的公司开始将开源作为其战略计划的一部分，这可能会给潜在的采用者灌输大量的怀疑态度。 当项目由公司运营时，有关项目未来的决策可能（但不一定）对最终用户变得不透明。 也就是说，企业赞助的项目也可能有能力以独立项目无法做到的方式关注安全性、开发人员体验和可用性，因为它们可以利用公司的时间、人才和财富。

通过识别项目的隶属关系，无论是公司、政府、非营利组织还是个人，它允许消费者根据自己的战略和价值观做出明智的决定。

一个例子是TypeScript与微软的公开合作。

三、资金来源

虽然开源是免费的（ 就像小狗一样），但它绝对不是免费的。 从带宽和基础设施到时间，成本可能会增加。 对于希望通过与开源软件集成来构建产品的人来说，切换组件的价格可能很高，因为项目之间的互操作性很少。 揭示项目如何支持自身提供了双重好处：1) 决定一项技术的人可以评估项目是否符合他们的风险状况，以及 2) 当前的集成商可以清楚地看到项目何时面临资金耗尽的风险。

此外， 财务透明度与开源价值观完全一致。

一个例子是由 GitHub 赞助商、OpenCollective 和 Patreon公开资助的 Homebrew。

正如log4j和Heartbleed等事件向我们展示的那样，为许多服务和应用程序提供关键功能的开源软件通常只由少数人维护。 此外，可能很难找到有关维护项目的人员的信息，这迫使用户通过邮件列表、提交历史等来搜索项目的元数据……以确定核心团队的规模。 团队的有效性可能取决于成员的数量，但还有许多其他变量会影响团队对其流程的管理程度。

发布核心团队成员的最新列表，即使除了用户名之外没有任何信息，也可以表明某个软件是否存在维护不足或有效维护不足的危险。 反过来，这也为用户提供了一个机会，让他们能够在关键的依赖关系中站出来并提供更积极的角色。

一个例子是公开分享其团队名单的 Font-Awesome。

透明度对每个人都有帮助

开放性不仅仅是代码； 它还涉及我们如何做出决定、我们如何支持自己、我们如何确定问题的优先级以及我们如何委派责任。

当然，项目认为重要的操作细节可能会有所不同，维护者会选择突出显示与他们最相关的那些。 处于开始阶段的项目可能还没有正式的治理模型或资金，而更成熟的项目可能具有各种已定义的角色和职责。

但是，只要有可能，提高工作完成方式的透明度就会为贡献者设定明确的期望，提高项目何时需要支持的可见性，并减少维护者集体肩上的工​​作量。

请留意我的下一篇文章，我们将在其中探讨如何将围绕交流的动态和约定纳入我们的开源社会模型。



原文:

This article is cross-posted from The Lead Dev and juliaferraioli.com.

From operating systems to CSS frameworks, open source gives creators the ability to accelerate development, learn from communities of experts, and incorporate thoroughly-reviewed code. At its heart, open source concerns freedoms relating to code, but an open source project is a complex system that happens to focus on code.

In my last article, we started to explore how we might move past the typical focus on the open source license and instead develop a social model that captures additional complexity. As a recap, we established that the technical model of open source describes what you can and cannot do with it, typically by way of the project's license, whereas the social model describes how open source interacts (or doesn’t) with the larger ecosystem and the people within it. That initial article addressed capturing the purpose of a project based on its specific goals within the open source ecosystem.

In this second piece, we will continue to develop the social model of open source by exploring the benefits of operational transparency, and outlining some key characteristics that maintainers should aim to make publicly available.

The benefits of operational transparency

A key benefit to open source is that anyone can examine the code they incorporate, but how the project operates is just as important to a project's success as the functionality itself. With open source making up an estimated 90% of today's modern technology, it’s increasingly critical to make operational information readily available to the public, so that you can empower contributors and users to evaluate projects based on the criteria important to them.

The operational characteristics of an open source project are often thought of as fundamentals to the teams behind them, and yet they’re rarely communicated well beyond the maintainers. This is a lost opportunity, as open source projects that clearly, concisely, and consistently articulate how they operate give contributors and users the social frameworks that they need to work well within the project.

Here are some high-priority details that projects should aim to make publicly available:

1. The decision-making model

A vital part of running an open source project is deciding how to run the project. While ‘governance’ is an ever-expanding term, its primary concern is around how decisions get made and who gets to make them. (The Open Source Way has a fantastic, comprehensive guidebook around open source governance.) Whether a project is managed by a benevolent dictator for life (BDFL) or is foundation-backed, clearly communicating the model in use benefits potential contributors and project alike.

An example is Node.js which openly shares that it follows a consensus seeking decision-making model.

2. The project's affiliations

As more companies start to create open source as part of their strategic initiatives, it can instill a healthy amount of skepticism in potential adopters. When a project is run by a corporation, decisions about the future of the project can (but not necessarily) become opaque to the end-user. That said, a corporate-sponsored project may also have the ability to focus on security, developer experience, and usability in a way that independent projects cannot, as they can leverage the time, talent, and treasure of the company.

By identifying the affiliation of a project, whether it be a company, government, non-profit, or individual, it allows consumers to make informed decisions based on their own strategy and values.

An example is TypeScript’s open affiliation with Microsoft.

3. The funding sources

While open source is free (as in puppy) to consume, it is most definitely not free to create. From bandwidth and infrastructure to time, costs can add up. For people looking to build products by integrating with open source software, the price of switching components can be high as interoperability between projects is rare. Exposing how the project supports itself provides a two-fold benefit: 1) people deciding on a technology can evaluate if the project fits their risk profile, and 2) current integrators can clearly see when a project becomes at risk of running out of capital.

Plus, financial transparency is right in line with open source values.

An example is Homebrew which has been openly funded by GitHub sponsors, OpenCollective, and Patreon.

4. The maintainers and the core team

As incidents such as log4j and Heartbleed have shown us, open source software that provides key functionality for many services and applications is often maintained by only a handful of people. Additionally, information about the people maintaining projects may be difficult to find, forcing users to trawl the project's metadata through mailing lists, commit history, etc… in order to determine the size of the core team. Effectiveness of a team may be dependent on the number of members, but there are many other variables that influence how well a team manages their processes.

Publishing an up-to-date list of core team members, even without any information beyond usernames, can signal when a piece of software is in danger of becoming under-maintained or effectively unmaintained. In turn, this opens up an opportunity for users to step up and offer to take a more active role within a crucial dependency.

An example is Font-Awesome which publicly shares its team list.

Transparency helps everyone

Openness is about more than just code; it is also about how we make decisions, how we support ourselves, how we prioritize issues, and how we delegate responsibilities.

Of course, the operational details that a project considers important may vary, and maintainers will choose to highlight those that are most relevant to them. A project in its beginning phase might not yet have a formalized governance model or funding, while a more established project may have a variety of defined roles and responsibilities.

But wherever possible, increasing transparency around how work gets done sets clear expectations for contributors, raises the visibility of when a project is in need of support, and reduces the amount of effort that falls on the maintainers' collective shoulders.

Keep an eye out for my next article where we'll explore how to incorporate dynamics and conventions around communication into our social model of open source.
item.author: juliaferraioli

item.title: 如何授权您的开源用户和贡献者 | How to empower your open source users and contributors
item.description:
本文由The Lead Dev和juliaferraioli.com交叉发布。

当开源的概念首次出现时，人们非常有意识地在项目上进行合作。 如果没有今天庞大的互联网和信息检索系统，在这些共享系统上工作的人（主要是研究人员）网络在很大程度上是通过口口相传而增长的。 如果您正在与机构之外的人一起开发软件，那么您可能会在他们查看一行代码之前直接与他们进行交流。

即使世界上越来越多的人可以访问互联网，查找信息也是一项挑战。 尽管如此，开始使用或为新项目做出贡献所需的努力仍然很高。

为了找到您需要的资源，例如源代码或文档（如果有的话），您通常必须联系项目的作者。 但最终，网络搜索得到了改进，随着SourceForge等托管和分发平台的兴起，查找有关如何使用和为开源做出贡献的信息变得更加容易。 基础设施、硬件和计算机科学的改进降低了实验成本，同时增加了可自由修改和分发软件的范围。

人们接受开源的障碍开始消失。

打破开源壁垒

新贡献者比以往任何时候都更容易访问开源，部分原因是BitBucket 、 GitHub和GitLab等平台推动的用户体验改进。 它们提供了一种发现新存储库并查看其源代码的方法。 这意味着您可以在深入投资之前浏览和了解项目。 它对资源有限的人（例如磁盘空间或网络带宽）有额外的好处，因为您不再需要克隆整个存储库（将文件复制到您的计算机）来查看其内容。

也就是说，开源体验仍有相当大的改进空间。

授权开源用户和贡献者的四种方法

上一次，我们通过概述为什么开源项目应该提供有关它们如何运作的信息来扩展开源的初始社会模型。 这一次，我们将探讨项目如何采用类似的方法来帮助用户和贡献者与他们互动。

技术文档早在第一行代码 之前就已经存在。 好的文档位于艺术与科学之间的交叉点，技术写作专业知识在开源项目中备受追捧。 为开源创建文档会带来一些挑战，包括需要文档的大量受众！ 项目可以降低参与障碍的最有效方法之一是不仅指定文档所在的位置，还指定可用的文档类型。

考虑一下 Python 编程语言设置的示例，它指向了几组针对不同技能水平和角色的文档。

虽然现在许多项目选择在托管代码的地方报告问题，但有些项目更喜欢使用开源或第三方解决方案，例如Bugzilla或Trac 。 无论项目在哪里报告问题并讨论它们，都非常清楚社区成员应该在哪里报告当前问题，这对他们来说是一种更轻松的体验。 作为一个附带好处，它通过减少他们需要做的重定向量来降低维护者的成本。

如果项目发布了有关如何提交问题的指南或模板，则可以加分！

维护者和用户之间的沟通频率对采用者来说越来越重要，并且可以成为做出技术决策的一个因素。 然而，促进贡献者和用户之间的对话表明了一种有利于项目社区所有参与者的深思熟虑和意图。 注重记录官方讨论渠道的项目鼓励健康社区的发展，让社区成员相互支持。

一个例子是 Apache Beam 如何列出其各种讨论论坛以及何时使用它们。

随着软件复杂性的增加，为开源做贡献的复杂性也在增加。 项目可能有特定于语言的样式指南，他们要求修改遵循，或者当新贡献者想要修复错误时必须遵守的流程。 虽然这些工作协议有时对潜在的贡献者来说似乎过于规范，但它们的存在是为了最大限度地提高每个相关人员的成功。 贡献者尽量减少浪费的精力，维护者尽量减少来回回答问题和请求更改评论的次数。

一般来说，设定这些期望可以提高士气！

与提供贡献者指南相同的是，记录架构开发应遵循的流程，例如 Bazel 如何呼吁如何对核心项目进行更大规模的更改。

思考

另一种思考开源的方式是通过授权的视角。 您有权查看您使用的软件的源代码。 您有权对其进行修改、分发和学习。 那么，为什么不将其作为合乎逻辑的结论，并为您的社区成员提供他们需要的信息，以便在您的开源项目中和在您的开源项目中高效工作呢？

这些只是您可以通过我们的开源社会模型考虑的一些以社区为中心的元素，尽管它们对于许多（如果不是大多数）项目都很常见。 下次您更新项目的 README 时，请查看它以查看您是否为社区提供了使他们成功的资源，无论是作为用户、贡献者还是未来的共同维护者。



原文:

This article is cross-posted from The Lead Dev and juliaferraioli.com.

When the concept of open source first emerged, people collaborated on projects very intentionally. Without today's vast internet and information retrieval systems, the network of people (largely researchers) working on these shared systems largely grew via word-of-mouth. If you were working on a piece of software with folks outside of your institution, you likely would have communicated with them directly prior to them ever viewing a single line of code.

Even as more of the world gained access to the internet, finding information was a challenge. Usenet, a distributed discussion platform that came out of University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and Duke University in 1980, arrived on the scene as a popular way to discover and communicate about software with freely available source code. Still, the effort needed to start using or contributing to a new project was high.

In order to find resources that you needed, such as source code or documentation (if any existed), you’d generally have to contact the author of the project. But eventually, web search improved, and with the rise of hosting and distribution platforms such as SourceForge it became much easier to find information about how to use and contribute to open source. Improvements in infrastructure, hardware, and computer science reduced the cost of experimentation and simultaneously increased the reach of freely modifiable and distributable software.

Barriers to people embracing open source started falling away.

Breaking down the barriers to open source

Open source is more accessible to new contributors than ever before, in part due to the user experience improvements pushed forward by platforms such as BitBucket, GitHub, and GitLab. They provide a way to discover new repositories and view their source. This means that you can browse and understand a project before becoming deeply invested. It has the added benefit for people with resource constraints, such as disk space or network bandwidth, because you no longer have to clone an entire repository (copy the files to your computer) in order to view its contents.

That said, there's still a fair amount of improvement that can be made to the open source experience.

Four ways to empower open source users and contributors

Last time, we extended the initial social model of open source by outlining why open source projects should provide information about how they operate. This time, we'll explore how projects can take a similar approach to helping users and contributors engage with them.

1. Documentation

Technical documentation existed long before the first line of code. Good documentation rests at that intersection between art and science, with technical writing expertise being highly sought-after in open source projects. Creating documentation for open source poses several challenges, including the sheer variety of audiences that need the docs! One of the most effective ways that a project can lower barriers to becoming involved is by specifying not only where documentation lives but also what type of documentation is available.

Consider the example set by the Python programming language, which points to several sets of documentation targeting different skill levels and roles.

2. Issues

While many projects these days choose to take reports of issues alongside where their code is hosted, some prefer to use an open source or third-party solution, such as Bugzilla or Trac. Wherever a project takes reports of issues and discusses them, making it very clear where community members should report current issues makes it an easier experience for them. As a side benefit, it lowers costs for maintainers by reducing the amount of redirection they need to do.

Jaeger sets a good example of this, by specifying where its issue trackers live, as well as where to file various types of issues.

Bonus points if a project publishes guides or templates for how to file issues!

3. Discussion

The frequency of communication between maintainers and users is of increasing importance to adopters, and can be a factor when making technology decisions. However, facilitating conversation between contributors and users alike signals a thoughtfulness and intentionality that benefits all participants in the project's community. Projects that make a point of documenting official channels for discussion encourage the growth of a healthy community, allowing community members to support each other.

An example is how Apache Beam lists its various discussion fora and when to use each.

4. Contributing

As the complexity of software increases, so does the complexity of contributing to open source. Projects may have language-specific style guidelines that they ask modifications to follow, or processes that must be adhered to when a new contributor wants to pick up a bug to fix. While these working agreements might on occasion seem overly prescriptive to prospective contributors, they are there to maximize the success of everyone involved. Contributors minimize the amount of wasted effort, and maintainers minimize the amount of back-and-forth answering questions and requesting changes in reviews.

Generally, setting these expectations improves morale!

Inkscape's CONTRIBUTING.md file outlines some general guidance as well as instructions about decision-making, building, testing, and documentation.

Along the same lines of providing contributor guidelines is documenting processes that architectural development should follow, such as how Bazel calls out how to make larger-scale changes to the core project.

Reflections

Another way to think about open source is through the lens of empowerment. You are empowered to see the source code of the software you use. You are empowered to modify it, distribute it, and learn from it. So why not take that to its logical conclusion and empower your community members with the information they need to be productive with and within your open source project?

These are just a few community-focused elements that you might consider through our social model of open source, though they are common to many if not most projects. (There are a few missing elements from our social model that we will address in the subsequent post.)

The next time you are updating the README for your project, review it to see if you are empowering your community with resources that will make them successful, whether as users, contributors, or future co-maintainers.

主要工具

生成 512 哈希

有两种方法可以使用SHA512 哈希函数生成器生成安全的 128 位字符密码，或者将其用作保护重要数据（如数字证书、互联网甚至保险交易）的密钥。

生成 384 哈希

生成 Md5 哈希

此工具对于生成可用于保护敏感数据（例如信用卡号、保险单号和其他金融交易）的唯一字符串至关重要。 在此处在线计算免费的 SHA-MD5 哈希。

元标签生成器

元标记是 HTML 标记产品，可提供有关您的站点的元数据，例如表示和其他类型的描述。 网络爬虫和搜索引擎利用这些元标记来帮助建立索引并在其搜索结果中提供相关和重要的信息。 使用我们的元标签生成器，您可以创建对 SEO 友好的元标签，这些标签不仅可以帮助网络索引识别您网站页面的内容，还可以帮助您提高互联网搜索排名。

推特卡片生成器

如果您想使用它们来宣传您的业务，则创建 Twitter 卡片涉及两个步骤。 第一步是为您要创建的 Twitter 卡创建元标记。 要使用它们，您必须先创建它们，然后将它们提交给 Twitter 以供批准。 审批流程从一份简短的申请开始，要求提供有关您和您的网站的一些基本信息。 请求添加到 Twitter 的白名单被称为白名单请求。 一旦 Twitter 获得批准，您网站的 Twitter 卡片元标记将被 Twitter 拾取并显示。
它非常简单，它为您提供了营销公司和吸引新客户的新途径。 打开图形生成器

开放图形标签为搜索引擎提供有关您网站的准确信息。 网站的开放图形协议包含有助于其在搜索结果中上升的数据。 具有开放图协议的网站将在 Facebook 等社交媒体网站上更加可见，从而获得更好的搜索引擎排名。 这正是网站管理员或所有者想要完成的。 免费的 SEO Agility Tools 现在提供了一个易于使用的在线开放图形生成器。 使用此 OG 标签生成器，您可以快速轻松地为您的网站或博客创建开放图。

您可以使用此Ping 站点地图工具通知支持快速 ping 您的站点地图的搜索引擎。 提交时需要符合 XML 站点地图协议的文件。 使用它有什么意义？ 可以通知搜索引擎新的站点地图 URL 或现有站点的更新，并请求他们通过发送快速 ping 重新抓取您的内容。
需要任何工具的帮助或支持？ 您可以随时与免费 SEO 敏捷工具团队取得联系。



原文:


Free SEO Agility Tools was founded in 2021 by SEO Agility Tools, a well-experienced (Professional background). The idea was to make high-quality SEO tools available to the general public. That's our ultimate goal: To create SEO tools those are equal to the paid ones, and make them available to everyone on the Internet at no cost. This is something we've been doing for the past (2021) and it's only just begun.

Major Tools

Generate 512 Hash

There are two ways to use the SHA512 hash function generator to generate a secure 128-bit char password or to use it as the key to protect important data like digital certificates, the internet, and even insurance transactions.

Generate 384 Hash

The SHA3-384 hash function generator at Free SEO Agility Tools generates a SHA3-384 hash which can be used as a secure 96-character password or used as a key to protect important data such as Insurance Health Company's data, market data, Monetary transactions, and much more. SHA3-384 hash function generator.

Generate 256 Hash

Highly secure 128 Hexadecimal passwords can be generated by using the SHAKE-256 hash function generator, which generates a SHAKE-256 hash for optimal asymmetric encryption padding.

Generate Md5 Hash

This tool is essential for generating a unique string that can be used to protect sensitive data, such as credit card numbers, insurance policy numbers, and other financial transactions. Compute Free SHA-MD5 Hash Online here.

Meta Tags Generator

Meta tags are HTML tag products that provide metadata about your site, such as representation and other types of description. Web crawlers and search engines make use of these Meta tags to help with indexing and to provide relevant and important information in their search results. Using our Meta Tags Generator, you can create SEO-friendly meta labels that not only assist web indexes in identifying the content of your website page but also aid in your internet searcher rankings.

Twitter Card Generator

There are two steps involved in creating Twitter cards if you want to use them to promote your business. The first step is to create the meta tags for the Twitter card that you want to create. To use them, you must first create them and then submit them to Twitter for approval. The approval process begins with a brief application that asks for some basic information about you and your website. Requesting to be added to Twitter's whitelist is known as a whitelist request. Once Twitter has given its approval, your website's meta tags for Twitter cards will be picked up and displayed by Twitter.
Twitter cards can now be used to promote your business. It's incredibly simple, and it gives you a new outlet for marketing your company and attracting new clients. Twitter card meta tags can be generated using a Twitter card generator or by writing and inserting the HTML code into your website's code. This tool by Free SEO Agility Tools lets you tweet a variety of content—photos and videos included—freely.

Open Graph Generator

Open graph tags give search engines accurate information about your website. A website's open graphs protocol contains data that aids in its ascent in the search results. A website with an open graph protocol will be more visible on social media sites like Facebook and get better search engine rankings as a result. It's exactly what a webmaster or owner wants to accomplish. Free SEO Agility Tools now offers an online open graph generator that is easy to use. Using this OG tag generator, you can quickly and easily create an open graph for your website or blog.

Ping Sitemap

You can use this Ping Sitemap tool to notify search engines that support fast ping of your Sitemap. XML Sitemaps Protocol compliant files are required for submission. What's the point of using it? It is possible to notify search engines of a new Sitemap URL or an update to an existing one and request that they re-crawl your content by sending a fast ping.
Need help or support with any of the tools? You can always get in touch with the Team of Free SEO Agility Tools.

item.title: Golang 中的 Websockets 不到 5 分钟。 | Websockets in Golang under 5 Minutes.
item.description:
首先构建一个简单的 HTTP 服务器，只要我们在端口 8001 上点击它就会返回 Hello Devs。我们还将定义一个简单的 HTTP 端点，它将作为我们将创建的 WebSocket 端点的基础：

升级 HTTP 连接

要创建 WS 端点，我们需要将标准 HTTP 连接升级为 WS 连接。 我们将为此使用gorilla/websocket包。

这将保存诸如我们的 WS 连接的读取和写入缓冲区大小之类的信息：

这将确定是否允许来自不同域的传入请求连接，如果不允许连接，它们将遇到 CORS 错误。 现在，我们将简单地返回 true。

我们现在可以开始尝试使用 upgrader.Upgrade() 函数升级传入的 HTTP 连接，该函数将接收 Response Writer 和指向 HTTP 请求的指针，并向我们返回指向 WebSocket 连接的指针，如果失败则返回错误升级。

开始聆听

连接客户端后，我们可以将 WS 传递给 reader 函数。

一个简单的阅读器功能，可以回显每条传入的消息：

就是这样！ 我们刚刚在 Go 中创建了一个 WS 端点。

进一步阅读：

在这里找到代码：
https://gist.github.com/akshanshgusain/f2a797bc593136fd3484edb6d7401c50 https://gist.github.com/akshanshgusain/f2a797bc593136fd3484edb6d7401c50



原文:

Start off by building a simple HTTP server that returns Hello Devs whenever we hit it on port 8001. We’ll also define a simple HTTP endpoint that will act as the base of the WebSocket endpoint that we’ll be creating:

Upgrading the HTTP Connection

To create a WS endpoint we need to upgrade a standard HTTP connection to a WS connection. We'll be using gorilla/websocket package for this.

1. Define the Upgrader

This will hold information such as the Read and Write buffer size for our WS connection:

2. Take care of the CORS

This will determine whether or not an incoming request from a different domain is allowed to connect, and if it isn’t they’ll be hit with a CORS error. For now, we'll simply return true.

3. Upgrading the Connection

We can now start attempting to upgrade the incoming HTTP connection using the upgrader.Upgrade() function which will take in the Response Writer and the pointer to the HTTP Request and return us with a pointer to a WebSocket connection, or an error if it failed to upgrade.

Start Listening

Once the client is connected we can pass our WS to a reader function.

*A simple echo reader: *
A simple reader function that echos back every incoming message:

And that's it! We just created a WS endpoint in Go.

Further reading:

https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/million-websockets-and-go-cc58418460bb/

Find the code here:

item.title: 在 Dart/Flutter 中使用正则表达式（RegExp） | Using Regular Expressions(RegExp) in Dart/Flutter
item.description:
博卢瓦提夫

发表于简介 创建正则表达式模式

字符组\d , \w , \s , \D , \W , \S , .


字符范围[] , [^]
重复和可选字符* , + , ? 分组子表达式()
选择模式|
单词和字符串边界\b ， ^ ， $
命名组子表达式(?<>)创建 RegExp 模式中的参数（多行、区分大小写、Unicode、dotAll） 转义特殊字符 贪婪匹配

正则表达式和函数的示例

介绍

正则表达式是用于匹配字符串中的字符组合的模式。 正则表达式在大多数编程语言中无处不在，如果您将其理解为一种，则可以轻松地将其应用于其他语言。 也就是说，语法可能存在一些差异。
在 dart 中，它们以RegExp类的形式出现。

创建正则表达式模式

RegExp regExp = RegExp ( r'hello' ); // hasMatch checks if a pattern matches a string, returns bool regExp . hasMatch ( 'hello' ); // true regExp . hasMatch ( 'hello John' ); // true regExp . hasMatch ( 'hello Amit' ); // true regExp . hasMatch ( 'hell no' ); // false

通过使用原始字符串（以 r 为前缀的字符串，例如 r'I am a raw string'、r'12345String' 等）创建正则表达式。 例如： RegExp(r'I am a RegExp'), RegExp(r'12345String'), RegExp(r'[0-9]\d+ab99')等。RegExp 将匹配尽可能多的字符，因此只要图案满足。

字符组\d , \w , \s , \D , \W , \S , .

字符组是表示其组中许多字符之一的符号，例如
字符范围[] , [^]

您可能还想创建自己的character group ； 您可以通过将所有可能要匹配的字符放在方括号（[]）中来做到这一点，例如RegExp(r'[abfky]') ，这将完全匹配括号中的一个字符（即'a'或“b”或“f”或“k”或“y”）。
如果要包含的字符在ASCII table中彼此跟随，则可以使用-字符，例如对于匹配除 '9' 之外的所有数字以及小写英文字母表的正则表达式，您将具有： RegExp(r'[0-8a-z]')

此外，您可能希望创建一个字符组，其中仅包含不在括号中的字符； 您可以通过在字符前添加插入符号 ('^') 来执行此操作。 例如
示例代码

RegExp range1 = RegExp ( r'[aY]' ); // Matches only 'a' or 'Y' range1 . hasMatch ( 'a' ); // true range1 . hasMatch ( 'Y' ); // true range1 . hasMatch ( 'y' ); // false range1 . hasMatch ( 'b' ); // false RegExp range2 = RegExp ( r'[^aY]' ); // Matches all characters except 'a' or 'Y' range2 . hasMatch ( 'a' ); // false range2 . hasMatch ( 'Y' ); // false range2 . hasMatch ( 'y' ); // true range2 . hasMatch ( 'b' ); // true RegExp range3 = RegExp ( r'[c-f2-5]' ); // Matches only one of 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', '2', '3', '4', '5' range3 . hasMatch ( 'b' ); // false range3 . hasMatch ( 'f' ); // true range3 . hasMatch ( '4' ); // true RegExp range4 = RegExp ( r'[^c-f2-5]' ); // Matches all characters except 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', '2', '3', '4', '5' range4 . hasMatch ( 'b' ); // true range4 . hasMatch ( 'f' ); // false range4 . hasMatch ( '4' ); // false

特殊字符，如. 重复和可选字符* , + , ? 可选字符? 您可能正在尝试匹配单词“color”或“color”，即该单词的美国和英国版本； 字母“u”是可选的。 匹配任一单词的正则表达式是RegExp(r'colou?r') ；

一个或多个+ ：匹配一个或多个出现的字符。 例如

如果您尝试匹配任意数量的数字（一位或多位），则正则表达式将为RegExp(r'\d+') ，即一位或多位数字

零个或多个* ：
这表示字符出现零次或多次。 例如RegExp(r'\w*')将匹配一个或多个字母数字字符。

精确范围{i,j} ：
这在尝试匹配特定范围内出现的字符时很有用，例如，对于 8 到 15 位数字，正则表达式将是RegExp(r'\d{8,15}') ，最小出现次数是第一个，最大出现次数第二（包括两者）。 不要在花括号中的任何位置放置空格

开放范围{i,}
这对于匹配出现 i 次或更多次的字符很有用，例如RegExp(r'\d{10,}')将匹配 10 位或更多位。

精确数{i} ：
这在尝试匹配特定数量的出现时很有用，例如，如果您尝试匹配 10 位数字，例如电话号码，正则表达式将为RegExp(r'\d{10}')

示例代码

RegExp optional = RegExp ( r'flavou?r' ); // matches 'flavour' or 'flavor' optional . hasMatch ( 'flavour' ); // true optional . hasMatch ( 'flavor' ); // true optional . hasMatch ( 'flavr' ); // false RegExp oneOrMore = RegExp ( r'hi+' ); // matches 'hi' or 'hii' or 'hiii'... oneOrMore . hasMatch ( 'hiiii' ); // true oneOrMore . hasMatch ( 'h' ); // false RegExp zeroOrMore = RegExp ( r'Sample\d*' ); // matches 'Sample', 'Sample1', 'Sample21', 'Sample456',... zeroOrMore . hasMatch ( 'Sample' ); // true zeroOrMore . hasMatch ( 'Sample468' ); // true zeroOrMore . hasMatch ( 'Sampld' ); // false RegExp range = RegExp ( r'\d{2,4}' ); // matches two, three or four digits, eg '12', '458', '7857' range . hasMatch ( '457' ); // true range . hasMatch ( '4' ); // false RegExp openRange = RegExp ( r'\d{5,}' ); // matches five or more digits openRange . hasMatch ( '4789' ); // false openRange . hasMatch ( '24789' ); // true RegExp precise = RegExp ( r'\w{4}' ); // Matches four alphanumeric characters, eg 'look', 'ball', 'boy1', '2002' precise . hasMatch ( 'walk' ); // true precise . hasMatch ( 'wal' ); // false

分组子表达式()

通过将子表达式的字符放在括号（'（）'）中来对子表达式进行分组。 分组子表达式有多种用途，但最重要的可能是重复和可选字符的应用（上一个子主题）。 除了重复一个字符，多个字符作为一个组子表达式可以重复或设为可选，例如RegExp(r'(ha)+')将匹配 1 次或多次出现的子表达式 'ha'。 示例代码

RegExp sub1 = RegExp ( r'boo(hoo)*' ); // Matches zero or more occurrences of 'hoo', ie would match 'boo', 'boohoo', 'boohoohoo',... sub1 . hasMatch ( 'boo' ); // true sub1 . hasMatch ( 'boohoo' ); // true sub1 . hasMatch ( 'boohoohoo' ); // true sub1 . hasMatch ( 'hoo' ); // false RegExp sub2 = RegExp ( r'What would you( like to)? say' ); // ' like to' is optional. This would match 'What would you like to say' and also 'What would you say' sub2 . hasMatch ( 'What would you like to say' ); // true sub2 . hasMatch ( 'What would you say' ) // true sub2 . hasMatch ( 'What you say' ); // false RegExp sub3 = RegExp ( r'(Meow ?){3,5}' ); // This would match between 3 and 5 occurrences of 'Meow ' sub3 . hasMatch ( 'Meow' ); // false sub3 . hasMatch ( 'Meow Meow Meow' ); // true sub3 . hasMatch ( 'Meow Meow Meow Meow' ); // true

选择模式|

有时您的意图可能是匹配字符或子表达式的几个选项中的任何一个； 在这种情况下，您使用管道 ('|') 符号。 选择模式适用于范围模型，即选项扩展到子表达式或字符的范围。 例如

示例代码

RegExp fruit = RegExp ( r'orange|banana|pineapple|water melon|apple' ); // This would match one of 'orange', 'banana', 'pineapple', 'water melon' and 'apple' RegExp regExp1 = RegExp ( r'1 girl|(2|3|4) girls' ); // This would match one of '1 girl', '2 girls', '3 girls', '4 girls'

单词和字符串边界\b , ^ , '$'

默认情况下，正则表达式会在第一次出现的任何地方找到匹配项，无论是在字符串的结尾、开头或中间，还是单词的开头、中间或结尾。 决定匹配发生的位置和方式是使用单词和字符串边界。

字边界\b ：
单词边界 ( \b ) 放置在单词的开头或/和结尾，以确保匹配只发生在字符串中单词的开头或/和结尾。 例如

RegExp word1 = RegExp ( r'\bstand\b' ); // matches a string that has a separate word 'stand' word1 . hasMatch ( 'I understand' ); // false word1 . hasMatch ( 'You have understanding' ); // false word1 . hasMatch ( 'I am standing' ); // false word1 . hasMatch ( 'I stand' ); // true RegExp word2 = RegExp ( r'\bstand' ); // matches a string that contains 'stand' at the beginning of a word word2 . hasMatch ( 'I understand' ); // false word2 . hasMatch ( 'You have understanding' ); // false word2 . hasMatch ( 'I am standing' ); // true word2 . hasMatch ( 'I stand' ); // true RegExp word3 = RegExp ( r'stand\b' ); // matches a string that contains 'stand' at the end of a word word3 . hasMatch ( 'I understand' ); // true word3 . hasMatch ( 'You have understanding' ); // false word3 . hasMatch ( 'I am standing' ); // false word3 . hasMatch ( 'I stand' ); // true

字符串边界^ ， $ ：
字符串边界表示匹配发生在字符串的开头或结尾。 例如

RegExp string1 = RegExp ( r'^stand$' ); // Matches a string that contains only 'stand' string1 . hasMatch ( 'stand now' ); // false string1 . hasMatch ( 'now stand' ); // false string1 . hasMatch ( 'stand' ); //true RegExp string2 = RegExp ( r'^stand' ); // Matches a string that starts with 'stand' string2 . hasMatch ( 'stand now' ); // true string2 . hasMatch ( 'now stand' ); // false string2 . hasMatch ( 'stand' ); // true RegExp string3 = RegExp ( r'stand$' ); // Matches a string that ends with 'stand' string3 . hasMatch ( 'stand now' ); // false string3 . hasMatch ( 'now stand' ); // true string3 . hasMatch ( 'stand' ); // true

命名组子表达式(?<>)

这是 dart 中正则表达式的特点之一。 命名组子表达式对匹配的内容没有影响，其本质是能够通过名称访问特定的匹配项。 例如

RegExp named = RegExp ( r'(?<number>\d+) (?<animal>cat|dog|cow|pig)s?' ); // Here there are two named group subexpressions; 'number' and 'animal' ('s' is optional) // RegExpMatch: regular expression matches, may be null(if no match) RegExpMatch ? match1 = named . firstMatch ( '5 dogs' ); match1 ?. namedGroup ( 'number' ); // '5' match1 ?. namedGroup ( 'animal' ); // 'dog' RegExpMatch ? match2 = named . firstMatch ( '10 cats' ); match2 ?. namedGroup ( 'number' ); // '10' match2 ?. namedGroup ( 'animal' ); // 'cat' RegExpMatch ? match3 = named . firstMatch ( '1 pig' ); match3 ?. namedGroup ( 'number' ); // '1' match3 ?. namedGroup ( 'animal' ); // 'pig'

创建 RegExp 对象的参数（multiLine、caseSensitive、unicode、dotAll）

在此之前，我们一直在创建仅使用原始字符串参数 ( RegExp(r'example') ) 的RegExp对象，但隐含地设置了 4 个其他参数：multiLine、caseSensitive、unicode 和 dotAll。 因此，当您创建 RegExp 对象时： RegExp(r'example') ，您会得到： RegExp(r'example', multiLine: false, caseSensitive: true, unicode: false, dotAll: false) 。

当为 false 时，字符串边界字符^和$匹配整个字符串的开头和结尾。 当为 false 时， ^和$匹配行的开头和结尾。 例如

RegExp nonMulti = RegExp ( r'end$' ); // multiline: false String testString = 'This is the end \n This is another line' ; nonMulti . hasMatch ( testString ); // false multi . hasMatch ( testString ); // true

当为 false 时，字母字符将匹配其小写和大写形式，“a”将匹配“a”和“A”，“B”将匹配“b”和“B”，等等。例如

RegExp sensitive = RegExp ( r'This is a pattern' ); // caseSensitive: true RegExp insensitive = RegExp ( r'This is a pattern' , caseSensitive: false ); sensitive . hasMatch ( 'This Is A Pattern' ); // false insensitive . hasMatch ( 'This Is A Pattern' ); // true

当为 false 时，正则表达式只匹配基本的 ASCII 字符。

 RegExp nonUnicode = RegExp ( r'\u{0221}' ); // unicode: true RegExp unicode = RegExp ( r'\u{0221}' , caseSensitive: false ); nonUnicode . hasMatch ( 'ȡ' ); // false unicode . hasMatch ( 'ȡ' ); // true

为假时， . 字符 除了换行符（\n）之外的任何字符；
当为真时， . 匹配任何字符，包括换行符

RegExp nonDotAll = RegExp ( r'^.+$' ); // dotAll: false RegExp dotAll = RegExp ( r'^.+$' , dotAll: true ); String testString = 'This is the end \n This is another line' ; nonDotAll . hasMatch ( testString ); // false dotAll . hasMatch ( testString ); // true

转义特殊字符

特殊字符包括\ 、 ^ 、 $ 、 ? 有时您可能希望直接匹配这些字符中的任何一个，为此您可以通过在字符前面加上反斜杠 ( \ ) 来转义字符的特殊含义，例如，要匹配$ ，您可以改写\$ ，即RegExp(r'\$') ，这将匹配字符串中的美元字符（'$'）。

 // This would match a string like a string like '$500.26' // '\$' escapes the special meaning of end of string and matches '$' // '\d+' means 1 or more digit characters // '\.' escapes the special meaning of '.'(matching any string except newline) and matches only '.' // '\d{2}' means exactly 2 digit characters RegExp money = RegExp ( r'\$\d+\.\d{2}' ); money . hasMatch ( ' \$ 10578.43' ); // true ('$' is also a special character in dart)

贪心匹配

默认情况下，匹配符号（ + 、 * 、 ? 、 {i,j} ）的正则表达式匹配尽可能多的字符，这称为“贪婪匹配”，例如 RegExp(r'\w+').hasMatch('Hippotamus')将匹配整个字符串 'Hippotamus'，即使第一个字母 'H' 足以满足正则表达式。 匹配尽可能少的字符（使匹配成功），这称为非贪婪匹配，您只需要附加问号符号（'？'），到你想匹配的正则表达式的部分非- 贪婪地，例如 RegExp(r'\w+?').hasMatch('Hippotamus') 只会匹配 'H'，因为这足以使匹配成功。 下面的代码片段就是一个例子：

 String testString = 'Superman45' ; final greedy = RegExp ( r'^(?<letters>\w+)(?<digits>\d*)$' ); // Greedy matching on the 'letters' group subexpression final greedyMatch = greedy . firstMatch ( 'Superman45' ); greedyMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'letters' ); // 'Superman45' // Matches whole string greedyMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'digits' ); // '' // The digits are not extracted final nonGreedy = RegExp ( r'^(?<letters>\w+?)(?<digits>\d*)$' ); // Non-greedy matching on the 'letters' group subexpression final nonGreedyMatch = nonGreedy . firstMatch ( 'Superman45' ); nonGreedyMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'letters' ) // 'Superman' nonGreedyMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'digits' ); // '45'

有意了解您是尝试匹配尽可能少的字符还是尽可能多的字符很有用。 对于“尽可能多”使用： * , + , {} , ? 正则表达式和字符串的有用方法

正则表达式方法

签名：RegExpMatch？ 这会在 testString 中查找第一个匹配的事件并将其作为 RegExpMatch 返回，如果未找到匹配则返回 null。

签名：Iterable RegExp.allMatches(String testString, [int startIndex = 0])

正则表达式匹配
如果您打算使用 firstMatch 或 allMatches 方法来提取命名子表达式，您应该使用(?<name>)命名您计划匹配的组表达式，如“命名组子表达式”部分所示。

 RegExp regExp = RegExp ( r'(?<record>(?<number>\d+) (?<animal>cat|dog|cow|pig)s?)' ); String records = '5 cats, 4 dogs, 9 cows and 15 pigs' ; RegExpMatch ? firstMatch = regExp . firstMatch ( records ); // Finds only first match print ( firstMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'record' )); // '5 cats' print ( firstMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'number' )); // '5' print ( firstMatch ?. namedGroup ( 'animal' )); // 'cat' print ( firstMatch ?[ 0 ]); // '5 cats' (whole match) print ( firstMatch ?[ 1 ]): // '5 cats' (first subexpression) print ( firstMatch ?[ 2 ]): // '5' (second subexpression) print ( firstMatch ?[ 3 ]): // 'cat' (third subexpression) Iterable < RegExpMatch > allMatches = regExp . allMatches ( records ); // Finds all matches for ( RegExpMatch match in allMatches ) { print ( match . namedGroup ( 'record' )) // '5 cats', '4 dogs', '9 cows', '15 pigs' print ( match . namedGroup ( 'number' )) // '5', '4', '9', '15' print ( match . namedGroup ( 'animal' )) // 'cat', 'dog', 'cow', 'pig' } Iterable < RegExpMatch > allMatchesWithStartIndex = regExp . allMatches ( records , 8 ); // Finds all matches from index 8 for ( RegExpMatch match in allMatchesWithStartIndex ) { print ( match . namedGroup ( 'record' )) // 4 dogs', '9 cows', '15 pigs' print ( match . namedGroup ( 'number' )) // '4', '9', '15' print ( match . namedGroup ( 'animal' )) // 'dog', 'cow', 'pig' }

在这种情况下，不需要命名组子表达式，例如

RegExp regExp = RegExp ( r'\d(\d(\d)(\d))(\d(\d(\d)(\d)))\d' ); RegExpMatch ? match = regExp . firstMatch ( '012345678' ); print ( match ?[ 0 ]); // '012345678' print ( match ?[ 1 ]); // '123' print ( match ?[ 2 ]); // '2' print ( match ?[ 3 ]); // '3' print ( match ?[ 4 ]); // '4567' print ( match ?[ 5 ]); // '567' print ( match ?[ 6 ]); // '6' print ( match ?[ 7 ]); // '7'

字符串方法

这类似于replaceFirst方法，除了替换String之外，有一个函数接受一个Match对象并返回一个String

例如
RegExp regExp = RegExp ( r'(\w+)\s(\w+)' ); // Matches a name String lastFirst = 'Smith John' ; String firstLast = lastFirst . replaceFirstMapped ( regExp , ( Match m ) => ' ${m[2]} ${m[1]} ' ); print ( firstLast ); // 'John Smith'

这就像replaceFirstMapped方法一样，而是替换字符串中所有匹配项。 例如

RegExp regExp = RegExp ( r'(\w+)\s(\w+)' ); // Matches a name String lastFirst = 'Smith John \n Nakamura Hikaru \n Adebayo Peter \n Ma Long' ; String firstLast = lastFirst . replaceAllMapped ( regExp , ( Match m ) => ' ${m[2]} ${m[1]} ' ); print ( firstLast ); // 'John Smith\nHikaru Nakamura\nPeter Adebayo\nLong Ma'

正则表达式和函数的例子

/// Extracts file extension from string String ? extractExt ( String fileName ) { final pattern = RegExp ( r'\.(?<ext>[0-9a-zA-Z]+)$' ); final match = pattern . firstMatch ( fileName ); return match ?. namedGroup ( 'ext' ); } extractExt ( 'verygoodfile.dart' ); // 'dart' /// Validates email string, *non ascii characters are not accepted* bool isValidEmail ( String email ) { final pattern = RegExp ( r'^[a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9-]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)*$' ); return pattern . hasMatch ( email . trim ()); } /// Password is valid if it has an uppercase, lowercase, number, symbol and has at least 8 characters bool isPasswordValid ( String ? password ) { final containsUpperCase = RegExp ( r'[AZ]' ). hasMatch ( password ); final containsLowerCase = RegExp ( r'[az]' ). hasMatch ( password ); final containsNumber = RegExp ( r'\d' ). hasMatch ( password ); final containsSymbols = RegExp ( r'[`~!@#$%\^&*\(\)_+\\\-={}\[\]\/.,<>;]' ). hasMatch ( password ); final hasManyCharacters = RegExp ( r'.{8,128}' , dotAll: true ). hasMatch ( password ); // This is variable return containsUpperCase && containsLowerCase && containsNumber && containsSymbols && hasManyCharacters ; }
